description:
Emit SyncReport `deleted` (or `updated` to status=deferred, depending
on policy) when a previously-mirrored GH issue vanishes from the API
(404 on GET /repos/o/n/issues/N for a number stored on a balls task).
Behavior driven by config.on_external_delete:
- "deferred" (default): emit `updated` with status="deferred" and
  add_note="GH issue #N no longer found"; the balls task survives,
  flagged for operator attention.
- "closed": emit `updated` with status="closed" and the same note;
  the balls task archives.
- "noop": emit nothing; the orphan mapping persists until manually
  cleared.

WHY default deferred and not closed: "set aside with intent to
revisit" (SKILL.md "Deferred vs closed") describes the operator's
position after an external delete better than closed does. Closed
means "we decided not to do this / we delivered this"; deferred is
"the external state surprised us; come back to it." Operator can
flip it to closed manually if they conclude the work isn't coming
back. Closed-by-default would archive work without operator review.

ACCEPTANCE:
- Each policy branch tested via mockito 404 response on the issue
  fetch; balls task ends in the documented status.
- A mass-delete shaped attack (every issue 404s) is bounded by the
  same bl-4673 backstops as B4c's creates-flood.
- 100% coverage; under 300 lines per file.
title: Pull: deleted entries (handle GH issue disappearance per on_external_delete policy)
